session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
<local-command-stdout>Set model to [1mDefault (Opus 4.6 · Most capable for complex work)[22m</local-command-stdout>
how would we change our ci pipelien so that we speculatively execute all tests in parallel with fix-formatting, and then if fix-formatting fails that tests start over
session.assistant: Let me explore the CI pipeline and understand the current structure.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 417 chars]...xisting CI documentation
Be thorough - read the full workflow files and Makefile targets.

session.tool_result:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile my findings into a clear report.
## CI Pipeline Summary for the Erk Project
I've thoroughly analyzed the CI pipeline by examining the workflows, Makefile, and documentation. Here's what I found:
### Documentation Resources **Key docs in `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/docs/learned/ci/`:** - `job-ordering-strategy.md` - Three-tier CI architecture - `ci-iteration.md` - devrun agent pattern and iteration workflow - `formatting-workflow.md` - Formatter decision tree (ruff vs prettier) - `tripwires.md` - Critical CI patterns and anti-patterns
### Primary CI Workflow: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
The repo-local CI uses a **three-tier architecture**:
#### **Tier 1: Gate Job** - **`check-submission`** (no dependencies) - Checks if PR is draft or labeled `erk-plan-review` - Outputs `skip` flag consumed by all downstream jobs - Prevents wasted compute on unready PRs - Also checks that `.erk/impl-context/` is not in tree
#### **Tier 2: Auto-Fix Boundary** - **`fix-formatting`** (depends only on `check-submission`) - Runs BEFORE parallel validation jobs (intentional design) - Executes three formatters sequentially: 1. `make docs-fix` (erk docs sync) 2. `uv run ruff format` (Python formatting)
... [99 lines omitted] ...
- Runs only on failure - Uses Haiku model to summarize CI failures for developers 1. Lint → Format Check → Type Check (fast, catch errors early) ci-summarize (on failure only)
session.assistant: Let me read the key files to understand the current design in detail.
session.tool_result:
1→name: ci 2→ 3→on: 4→ push: 5→ branches: [master] 6→ pull_request: 7→ types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review] 8→ workflow_dispatch: 9→ 10→permissions: 11→ contents: read 12→ 13→concurrency: 14→ group: ci-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }} 15→ cancel-in-progress: true 16→ 17→jobs: 18→ check-submission: 19→ if: github.event.pull_request.draft != true && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'erk-plan-review') 20→ runs-on: ubuntu-latest 21→ timeout-minutes: 5 22→ outputs: 23→ skip: ${{ steps.check.outputs.skip }} 24→ steps: 25→ - uses: actions/checkout@v4 26→ - name: Check for submission folder 27→ id: check 28→ uses: ./.github/actions/check-impl-context 29→ 30→ no-impl-context:
... [213 lines omitted] ...
39→ echo "::error::.erk/impl-context/ must be removed before merging. Run: git rm -rf .erk/impl-context/ && git commit && git push" 99→ echo "::error::Files need formatting or docs are out of sync" 109→ echo "::error::Formatting issues found (cannot auto-fix fork PRs)" 195→ # === CI Failure Summarization (Haiku) === 214→ (needs.format.result == 'failure' || 215→ needs.lint.result == 'failure' || 216→ needs.fix-formatting.result == 'failure' || 217→ needs.docs-check.result == 'failure' || 218→ needs.ty.result == 'failure' || 219→ needs.unit-tests.result == 'failure' || 220→ needs.integration-tests.result == 'failure' || 221→ needs.erk-mcp-tests.result == 'failure') 238→ - name: Summarize failures

1→--- 2→title: CI Job Ordering Strategy 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying CI job dependencies" 5→ - "adding new CI jobs" 6→ - "understanding fix-formatting gating" 7→ - "debugging why CI restarted after a push" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "adding a new CI job without including fix-formatting in its needs list" 10→ warning: "All validation jobs must depend on both check-submission and fix-formatting. Without fix-formatting, the job may run against unformatted code and fail unnecessarily." 11→ - action: "adding code review execution to ci.yml" 12→ warning: "Keep shipped review behavior in code-reviews.yml. Repo-local ci.yml should only own formatting, validation, and CI summaries." 13→--- 14→ 15→# CI Job Ordering Strategy 16→ 17→The repo-local CI workflow uses a three-tier architecture: a gate job controls entry, `fix-formatting` is the single mutating boundary, and seven validation jobs run against the cleaned state. Convention-based reviews run separately in `code-reviews.yml`. 18→ 19→<!-- Source: .github/workflows/ci.yml --> 20→ 21→## Three-Tier Architecture 22→ 23→``` 24→Tier 1: check-submission (gate) 25→ │ 26→Tier 2: fix-formatting (auto-fix, independent of parallel jobs) 27→ │ 28→Tier 3: ┌──────┬──────┬───────────┬────┬────────────┬─────────────┬──────────────┐ 29→ format lint docs-check ty unit-tests integration erk-mcp-tests 30→```
... [46 lines omitted] ...
61→- `pushed=false` — no changes needed (or error exit on master/fork) 69→**`ci-summarize`** depends on all validation jobs and runs only on failure. It uses a lightweight model to summarize CI failures for developers.
session.assistant:
Now I understand the full picture. The current design is:
``` check-submission → fix-formatting → [all 7 validation jobs in parallel] ```
The user wants speculative execution: start tests immediately without waiting for fix-formatting, and rely on `cancel-in-progress: true` to restart everything if fix-formatting pushes a fix.
Let me think about the design considerations and write the plan.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
Design a change to the erk CI pipeline (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) to speculatively execu...[truncated 2494 chars]...l work correctly
Please design the implementation plan with specific YAML changes needed.

session.tool_result:
Good. On master push or fork PRs, when formatting is needed, fix-formatting sets `pushed=false` and exits 1. In the current workflow, this means format/docs-check are skipped (dependency failed), but `fix-formatting` itself reports the error. This behavior is unchanged by our modification. The speculative jobs (lint, ty, tests) would still run independently, which is fine -- they provide useful signal regardless.
### Documentation Updates Required
The change modifies the established three-tier pattern, so documentation must be updated:
#### 1. `docs/learned/ci/job-ordering-strategy.md`
This is the primary document. The three-tier architecture description needs to be updated to reflect the two-track approach. The diagram should change to show speculative jobs branching directly from check-submission. The "Skip-on-Push Mechanism" section needs updating to explain that only `format` and `docs-check` use the `pushed` guard, while speculative jobs rely on `cancel-in-progress`.
The tripwire in this file: ```yaml - action: "adding a new CI job without including fix-formatting in its needs list" warning: "All validation jobs must depend on both check-submission and fix-formatting..." ```
Must be updated to something like: ```yaml - action: "adding a new CI job without considering fix-formatting dependency" warning: "Jobs affected by formatting/docs-sync must depend on fix-formatting. Jobs unaffected by formatting (lint, ty, tests) should depend only on check-submission for speculative execution." ```
#### 2. `docs/learned/ci/workflow-gating-patterns.md`
The "Output-Based Gating" section discusses the `pushed` output being checked by all downstream jobs. This needs updating to reflect that only `format` and `docs-check` check the `pushed` output.
#### 3. `docs/learned/ci/formatting-workflow.md`
The "Integration Points" section mentions "seven validation jobs run against the cleaned state." This needs a minor update.
... [34 lines omitted] ...
2. **ci-summarize needing fix-formatting in its needs array**: The ci-summarize job currently lists `fix-formatting` in its needs array. This should remain unchanged -- it needs to wait for all jobs including fix-formatting to complete before summarizing failures. No issue here.

tool_use.name: Write
tool_use.param:
# Speculative Test Execution in CI
## Context
Currently all 7 validation jobs wait for `...[truncated 2306 chars]...arts with all jobs passing 3. Confirm `ci-summarize` still triggers correctly on failures

session.assistant: The plan is straightforward — it's a small YAML change plus doc updates. The key insight is that test jobs (lint, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests) produce the same results regardless of formatting, so they can run speculatively. The existing `cancel-in-progress: true` mechanism already handles the restart case when fix-formatting pushes changes.

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param:
# Speculative Test Execution in CI
## Context
Currently all 7 validation jobs wait for `...[truncated 2306 chars]...arts with all jobs passing 3. Confirm `ci-summarize` still triggers correctly on failures

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/temporal-toasting-lollipop.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Speculative Test Execution in CI Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...

1→# Speculative Test Execution in CI 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→Currently all 7 validation jobs wait for `fix-formatting` to complete before starting (~1-3 min). Most test jobs (ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests, lint) are unaffected by formatting changes, so they waste time waiting. By running them speculatively in parallel with `fix-formatting`, we save 1-3 minutes of wall-clock time on every CI run where code is already formatted (the common case). If fix-formatting pushes changes, `cancel-in-progress: true` already cancels the old run and starts a fresh one. 6→ 7→## Design: Two-Track Architecture 8→ 9→``` 10→check-submission (gate) 11→ ├── fix-formatting ──→ format, docs-check (wait for clean code) 12→ └── lint, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests (speculative) 13→``` 14→ 15→**Format-sensitive jobs** (`format`, `docs-check`): Keep depending on `fix-formatting` — they'd fail on unformatted code. 16→ 17→**Speculative jobs** (`lint`, `ty`, `unit-tests`, `integration-tests`, `erk-mcp-tests`): Depend only on `check-submission`. Start immediately. If fix-formatting pushes, `cancel-in-progress` kills the old run and a new run starts with clean code. 18→ 19→## Changes 20→ 21→### 1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` 22→ 23→For each speculative job (`lint`, `ty`, `unit-tests`, `integration-tests`, `erk-mcp-tests`): 24→- Change `needs: [check-submission, fix-formatting]` → `needs: [check-submission]` 25→- Remove `needs.fix-formatting.outputs.pushed != 'true'` from the `if:` condition 26→ 27→Keep `format` and `docs-check` unchanged (still depend on `fix-formatting` + check `pushed`). 28→ 29→Keep `ci-summarize` unchanged (still depends on all jobs including `fix-formatting`). 30→
... [16 lines omitted] ...
45→3. Confirm `ci-summarize` still triggers correctly on failures
session.assistant:
## Speculative Test Execution in CI
### Context
Currently all 7 validation jobs wait for `fix-formatting` to complete (~1-3 min). Most test jobs are unaffected by formatting changes, so they waste time waiting. By running them speculatively in parallel with `fix-formatting`, we save 1-3 min on every CI run. If fix-formatting pushes changes, `cancel-in-progress: true` already cancels the old run and starts fresh.
### Design: Two-Track Architecture
``` check-submission (gate) ├── fix-formatting ──→ format, docs-check (wait for clean code) └── lint, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests (speculative) ```
**Format-sensitive jobs** (`format`, `docs-check`): Keep depending on `fix-formatting`.
**Speculative jobs** (`lint`, `ty`, `unit-tests`, `integration-tests`, `erk-mcp-tests`): Depend only on `check-submission`. If fix-formatting pushes, `cancel-in-progress` kills the old run.
### Changes
1. **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — For 5 speculative jobs: remove `fix-formatting` from `needs`, remove `pushed` check from `if:` 2. **`docs/learned/ci/job-ordering-strategy.md`** — Update architecture diagram and tripwire 3. **Minor doc updates** + `erk docs sync`

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'What would you like to do with this plan?', 'header': 'br:master', 'options...[truncated 279 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Branch slug: `speculative-ci-tests`
Summary: Run test jobs (lint, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests) speculatively in parallel with fix-formatting instead of waiting for it. Format-sensitive jobs (format, docs-check) still wait. Relies on existing cancel-in-progress to restart if fix-formatting pushes changes.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" --bra...[truncated 215 chars]... wait. Relies on existing cancel-in-progress to restart if fix-formatting pushes changes."
Save plan as draft PR via erk

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "plan_number": 9035, "plan_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9035", "title": "[erk-plan] Speculative Test Execution in CI", "branch_name": "plnd/speculative-ci-tests-03-09-0559", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/scratch/sessions/2cb52bdf-0493-4582-9008-856ac7fefb26/plan_snapshots/000001-1e6ef173"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get session file path for push

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec push-session --session-file "/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn-cod...[truncated 58 chars]...4582-9008-856ac7fefb26" --stage planning --source local --plan-id 9035 2>/dev/null || true
Push planning session for cross-machine learning
